CD01.title: Repository Integrity & Surface Hygiene
CD01.summary: Controls the active repository surface so ghost artifacts, stale residue, and polluted roots do not masquerade as live knowledge or valid output.
covers: Ghost or undeclared active artifacts
Root hygiene after audits and refactors
Archive boundaries between active and historical surfaces
excludes: Dead code semantics inside otherwise active files
CD01.graph_role: High-value graph hub for active-surface discoverability: if this domain drifts, the graph loses trust in what is live versus archival.

principles.action: Treat leftover backups, stale plan docs, spectral scripts, and orphan residue as governance debt, not harmless clutter.
Keep the root workspace clean after every audit or remediation pass so historical evidence never pollutes active context.
Prefer simple, auditable execution surfaces and remove temporary helpers unless they are reusable and documented.

CD02.title: Completeness & State Continuity
CD02.summary: Keeps the operational contract complete by making state, scope, evidence, and checkpoints explicit before and during execution.
covers: Missing required artifacts or incomplete operational contracts
State continuity across long runs
Preflight scope declaration and controlled execution boundaries
excludes: Naming clarity as a stylistic concern
Runtime telemetry and post-run evidence streams
CD02.graph_role: Bridge domain between doctrine and execution flow: it keeps principles connected to durable state instead of chat memory.

principles.action: Store state, evidence, checkpoints, and control signals outside the chat so the operational contract stays complete.
Batch predictable authorizations and clarifications before long runs so the control plane can execute without interruptions.
Declare scope, foreseeable impacts, and affected runners before execution, then record new work in backlog instead of reopening scope reactively.

CD03.title: Legibility & Causal Explainability
CD03.summary: Makes code, assertions, and protocol prose readable enough to expose causal structure instead of hiding it behind opaque naming or unexplained failure output.
covers: Descriptive names and low-noise topology
Actionable failure messages
Confidence-tagged, causally explicit protocol prose
excludes: General discourse style independent of evidence
CD03.graph_role: Semantic bridge between doctrine and graph readability: improves whether the graph edges and their surrounding prose are cognitively inspectable.

principles.action: Require failure messages that explain the discrepancy with enough clarity to debug without guesswork.
Prefer descriptive names and simple topology so purpose is visible at first glance.
Tag protocol claims with VERIFIED, INFERRED, or ASSUMED so readers can separate evidence from inference.

CD04.title: Code Vitality & Dead Surface
CD04.summary: Distinguishes active, load-bearing logic from inert, spectral, or ceremonial code surface so the repository does not accumulate dead operational mass.
covers: Dead or spectral executable surface
Residue that pretends to be active logic
Helpers that survive after their real load-bearing role is gone
excludes: Tree-level ghost files as a repository-integrity problem
CD04.graph_role: Candidate-orphan pressure valve: this domain questions whether a file exists because it is alive or because nobody removed it.

principles.action: Treat spectral scripts, stale helper layers, and dead refactor residue as a governance failure, not as harmless leftovers.
Temporary helpers must disappear unless they remain reusable, documented, and visibly load-bearing.
Use local static smell detection to expose low-quality or inert AI-generated code before it becomes accepted surface.

CD05.title: Structural Simplicity & Blast Radius
CD05.summary: Controls complexity, indirection, and fan-in so the system stays comprehensible and graph hubs receive the scrutiny their impact deserves.
covers: Spaghetti growth and deep indirection
Needless branching or provider-specific routing tangles
High fan-in hub review and blast-radius prioritization
CD05.graph_role: Primary graph-priority domain: this is where hubs stop being decorative and become audit-order signals.

principles.action: Centralize routing and fallbacks so the code does not grow provider-specific branching spaghetti.
Flatten structure when it reduces cognitive friction and removes needless indirection.
Review graph hubs first when the catalog changes because high fan-in nodes carry the largest impact radius.

CD06.title: Failure Handling & Recovery Paths
CD06.summary: Turns failure into a structured operational path with visible next steps, actionable diagnostics, and zero silent debt advancement.
covers: Angry-path discipline
Structured next steps and recovery paths
Warnings treated as operational errors until resolved
excludes: Security scanning as a separate concern
CD06.graph_role: Failure-oriented bridge node: links doctrine to what happens when the graph surfaces a real break instead of a decorative warning.

principles.action: Turn failure handling into a structured protocol with next steps, evidence, and a visible recovery path.
Treat warnings and known non-blocking findings as operational errors until they are fixed or explicitly blocked.
When behavior is stochastic, define sampling, thresholds, and repetition criteria instead of declaring correctness from one lucky run.

CD07.title: Boundary Hygiene & Anti-Theater
CD07.summary: Protects the system against ceremonial progress by enforcing minimal real exclusions, justified handoffs, and anti-theater guard design.
covers: Justified exclusions and minimal bypasses
Boundary discipline between doctrine, execution, and evidence
Anti-theater control over mocks, placeholders, and ceremonial guards
CD07.graph_role: Semantic hygiene hub: keeps graph links honest by forcing the repository to justify exceptions instead of normalizing theater.

principles.action: Allow exclusions only when they are minimal, justified, and real; false coverage is debt, not progress.
Require each new guard to break a real circularity and reduce the baseline instead of merely naming the problem.
Split broad coverage theater into discriminative checks so one guard never pretends to cover many unrelated failures.

CD08.title: Runtime & Data Security
CD08.summary: Protects live execution surfaces against secrets, dangerous operations, exfiltration, and security-sensitive runtime misuse.
covers: Secrets and dangerous operations in active code paths
Runtime misuse that can exfiltrate data or expand blast radius
Security scanning across code and infrastructure surfaces
excludes: Package freshness and yanked dependency posture
Agent jailbreak taxonomy as its own boundary class
CD08.graph_role: Security-critical bridge between doctrine and live execution: domain edges here should be treated as high-consequence surfaces.

principles.action: Scan repos, images, filesystems, and IaC for secrets, vulnerabilities, and misconfigurations before release.
Use local linting that catches hardcoded secrets, eval, and empty catch blocks as early runtime-security signals.
Intercept risky agent operations through a policy-enforcement layer instead of trusting every generated action.

CD09.title: Coverage Adequacy
CD09.summary: Asks whether active logic is actually covered by meaningful guards and whether new protection reduces real uncovered surface.
covers: Whether relevant behavior is covered at all
Coverage planning for high-risk or circularity-heavy areas
Adequate evaluation of stochastic behavior
excludes: Assertion quality inside an existing test
CD09.graph_role: Coverage-to-graph connector: domain edges here help determine which active graph surfaces remain unprotected.

principles.action: Validate dependency declarations before running coverage gates so missing imports do not fake a green test surface.
Each new guard must shrink a real uncovered circularity baseline rather than merely expand the list of names.
Audit stochastic behavior with declared sampling and thresholds instead of single-run optimism.

CD10.title: Test Falsifiability & Assertion Quality
CD10.summary: Makes tests fail for the right reason, with precise assertions and honest labeling when no physical gate can yet exist.
covers: Diagnostic assertion quality
Honest `DOC_ONLY` labeling for non-falsifiable lessons
Discriminative tests that fail for the claimed reason
CD10.graph_role: Falsifiability anchor: prevents the graph from confusing the existence of a test file with the existence of real protection.

principles.action: Keep assertion output precise and inspectable so a failing test explains the discrepancy instead of performing theater.
Mark non-falsifiable lessons as DOC_ONLY instead of pretending automatic coverage exists.
Avoid many-to-one guards that claim to protect many vices but cannot isolate the real failure.

CD11.title: Context Efficiency & Tokenomics
CD11.summary: Treats token spend, context hygiene, trimming, retrieval, and memory discipline as operational efficiency concerns subordinate to code quality.
covers: Token metering and spend visibility
Context hygiene, trimming, and memory discipline
Tooling that reduces rereads and noisy interaction
CD11.graph_role: Dense graph bridge between doctrine, telemetry, and tooling: one of the main semantic intersections in GS.

principles.action: Meter tokens before and during LLM calls so spend is visible before usage grows.
Unify provider routing, fallback, and cost tracking so efficiency and resilience are handled once.
Treat output trimming, context hygiene, and history externalization as first-class controls.
Use rate limiting to avoid quota surprises and reduce reactive token waste.
Prefer symbol-level retrieval so recovered context is correct instead of bloated by blind chunking.

CD12.title: Dependency & Supply-Chain Posture
CD12.summary: Keeps declared dependencies, package freshness, and third-party intake aligned with the real code surface and vulnerability posture.
covers: Declared versus imported dependency reconciliation
SCA and yanked-package posture
Third-party code and package intake discipline
CD12.graph_role: Supply-chain traceability hub: connects doctrine to package reality and prevents invisible third-party drift.

principles.action: Compare imports against declared dependencies and fail on missing, unused, transitive, or misplaced packages.
Audit packages and related build surfaces against vulnerabilities, secrets, and SBOM gaps before release.
Use current documentation sources to reduce reliance on obsolete or hallucinated package APIs.

CD13.title: Knowledge Canonicalization & Ingestion
CD13.summary: Preserves GS as a live, deduplicated doctrine by normalizing new lessons and forcing explicit uncertainty handling before they become canon.
covers: Canonical intake of new lessons
Deduplication and normalization before doctrine promotion
Explicit uncertainty and semantic consistency across docs
CD13.graph_role: Knowledge backbone of the graph: protects hub coherence by ensuring new nodes enter through normalized, deduplicated doctrine.

principles.action: Keep GS alive by absorbing lessons from the core project and satellites without contaminating doctrine with local tooling details.
Normalize, deduplicate, and record new lessons before folding them into the central knowledge base.
Audit protocol documents semantically to detect contradictions, stale references, and incompatible claims.
Keep an explicit uncertainty list so unverified claims are visible before they become doctrine.

CD14.title: Federation Drift & Version Parity
CD14.summary: Keeps shared state, concurrent work, and federated repos from diverging silently through disciplined parity and serialized coordination.
covers: Satellite and shared-state drift
Concurrent editing awareness
Serialized coordination for stateful Git operations
CD14.graph_role: Cross-repo coherence bridge: without this domain, graph-connected doctrine diverges from the federated surfaces that consume it.

principles.action: Check shared state and recent commits before editing so concurrent sessions do not overwrite each other silently.
Persist structured retrospectives before context resets so continuity survives across sessions and federated work.
Execute Git operations serially in orchestrations to avoid index locks, races, and inconsistent shared state.

CD15.title: Observability & Evidence Telemetry
CD15.summary: Makes runtime signals, costs, decisions, and evidence streams visible early enough to support intervention rather than decorative post-mortems.
covers: Live token and quality signals
Decision and event telemetry
Durable evidence streams and structured session exports
excludes: Debt state as a semantic category
CD15.graph_role: Graph observability hub: the domain that turns the graph from a static index into a measurable audit surface.

principles.action: Make cost visible before execution so token telemetry exists as evidence, not as a hindsight estimate.
Observe risky signals during execution, not after the fact, so monitoring can interrupt damage early.
Use explicit confidence tags so state claims are measurable against real evidence instead of decorative certainty.
Persist structured retrospective exports to a durable ledger before any context reset.

CD16.title: Discourse Rigor & Evidence Quality
CD16.summary: Disciplines protocol prose and agent-facing doctrine so claims stay evidence-backed, scoped, and resistant to ambiguity drift.
covers: Evidence-backed protocol writing
Ambiguity control in doctrine and handoffs
Explicit separation of verified facts from assumptions
CD16.graph_role: Narrative integrity layer for the graph: keeps semantic links surrounded by prose that is explicit, bounded, and auditable.

principles.action: Tag every state claim with explicit confidence so discourse communicates evidence quality directly.
Run semantic wiki-lint so documents do not drift into incompatible or contradictory claims.
Declare which claims were not mechanically verified in the current session instead of letting ambiguity pass as fact.
Declare scope, impacts, and out-of-scope follow-ups before execution so handoff prose remains bounded and honest.

CD17.title: Agent Boundary Security
CD17.summary: Protects the agent layer itself against jailbreaks, leakage, runaway loops, and unsafe persistent-memory or prompt-boundary failures.
covers: Prompt injection and jailbreak resistance
Prompt leakage and unsafe prompt-surface handling
Agent memory and context-boundary discipline
excludes: Generic secrets in ordinary code paths
CD17.graph_role: High-risk semantic perimeter: domain links here should be treated as trust-boundary edges, not ordinary knowledge references.

principles.action: Use metacognitive interrupts to curb tunnel vision and runaway loops before the agent crosses unsafe behavioral boundaries.
Intercept risky agent operations through policy hooks so boundary violations are blocked before execution.
Treat durable cross-session memory as a trust-boundary surface that requires source, date, and reconciliation discipline.
